text: In personalized FL, each client’s objective may not be aligned with the global objective. The resulting statistical heterogeneity across clients renders the global model inapplicable to any of the individual clients. Hence, it is essential to align the global update with the local objective at each client in order to train the client model towards the global objective. Motivated by this observation, we introduce a novel framework of FL, namely, Federated learning with Adaptive Local Aggregation (FedALA) which obtains the required information for the optimal client model in personalized FL. The core of the proposed framework is the Adaptive Local Aggregation (ALA) module, which appropriately integrates the global model shared from the server and the local model optimized by the client to the local optimization target, so as to obtain the local model’s initialization in each FL iteration. Experiment results on five commonly used datasets from the computer vision and natural language processing fields demonstrate that FedALA improves the test accuracy by up to 3.27% over eleven popular baselines. When the ALA module is used in other FL methods, the maximum improvement of test accuracy is up to 24.19%. Our code is available in https://github.com/TsingZ0/FedALA.
